Article Title: Kevin O’Leary: Not Mark Cuban: Shark Tank veteran flaunts $20 million Kobe Bryant and Michael Jordan Dual Logoman card necklace at Actor Awards 2026
Article Text:
Kevin O’Leary. Image via: Kevin Winter/ Getty Images

Kevin O’Leary shows off a whopping $20 million Kobe Bryant and Michael Jordan Dual Logoman card necklace



When did Kevin O’Leary purchase the Kobe Bryant and Michael Jordan Dual Logoman card?



The red carpet at the 2026 Actor Awards made headlines for several reasons, but one NBA memorabilia item caught the attention of everyone. Not Mark Cuban , but Shark Tank mainstay Kevin O’Leary arrived wearing a $20 million Kobe Bryant and Michael Jordan Dual Logoman card around his neck. The rare collectible, framed in diamonds and white gold, drew immediate attention as cameras flashed outside the Shrine Auditorium in Los Angeles.O’Leary attended the ceremony as part of the cast of Marty Supreme, nominated for Outstanding Performance by a Cast in a Motion Picture. Yet it was the weight of history resting on his chest that became the night’s talking point. The one-of-one trading card honoring two NBA legends turned a traditional awards arrival into a headline moment.The necklace featured a 1-of-1 2007-08 Upper Deck Exquisite Collection Dual Logoman Autograph card signed by Kobe Bryant and Michael Jordan.Set in 2.2 pounds of Tiffany white gold and surrounded by 100 carats of Tiffany diamonds, it was designed to stand out.On the red carpet, O’Leary told TSN Sports, “We brought Kobe back to L.A. That’s a one of one dual logo, man, signed with Jordan as well. It’s a $20 million card in 2.2 pounds of Tiffany white gold and 100 karats of Tiffany diamonds. I mean, we’re rockin’ it tonight, baby, that’s it.”He also explained the reason behind the tribute. “I brought this back because I want to bring Kobe back to LA on such a big night in the city,” he said.“It’s the first time we brought it out; I thought it would be great for the red carpet.”Hours earlier, he posted a video and said, “The weight of this thing is, like, two pounds of solid gold and diamonds,” before adding, “But it’s all for Kobe.”O’Leary purchased the card in August 2025 for $12.932 million alongside partners Matt Allen and Paul Warshaw. At the time, he spoke in detail about the mindset behind the acquisition.“The thing that’s amazing about the hobby is the relationship between a father or a mother and a daughter and a son, because if you look at collecting, everything you need to learn about entrepreneurship is baked into what happens when you start to collect cards: ‘Should I buy it? Will it go up in value? Should I sell it?’ These are the decisions that kids that are five and six years old are getting into,” he previously told Fox Business.He added, “And as a result, we got multiple generations, particularly father-sons that have been forever. So, I was really intrigued. And I said, ‘If we go to buy a card, we’re going to buy it. There’s no maybe. We’re gonna buy it.’ And Shyne said, ‘This is the No. 1 card on Earth.’ A lot of it has to do with that gold patch logoman. It’s mesmerizing.”O’Leary made clear he has no plans to sell. “What I do is build indexes. It’s a labor of love – it’s half art, half science. But what’s the emotional connection with the card? For me, with that card, it’s that gold logoman. It’s unique. It’s a piece unique.”The necklace has since been the talk of the town, and O’Leary revealed that it has been one of his greatest possessions.
Article Summary: Kevin O’Leary wore a $20 million Kobe Bryant and Michael Jordan Dual Logoman card around his neck. The rare collectible, framed in diamonds and white gold, drew immediate attention as cameras flashed outside the Shrine Auditorium in Los Angeles. O'Leary purchased the card in August 2025 for $12.932 million.

Article Title: “She's done with it”: LeBron James spilled beans on Savannah James’ true feelings on their daughter Zhuri James sports prospects
Article Text:
LeBron James, Savannah James, Zhuri James. Image via: Michael Buckner/Variety via Getty Images | Getty Images

LeBron James opens up about Savannah James ’ stance on Zhuri James’ athletic future



LeBron James with his daughter Zhuri Nova (Image via Getty)

Zhuri James shows off impressive basketball skills



The Los Angeles Lakers wrapped up a road win in San Francisco, but the postgame buzz centered on a family moment. Before tipoff against the Golden State Warriors at Chase Center, LeBron James shared the floor with his 11-year-old daughter, Zhuri James. Cameras caught her dribbling and shooting with ease. The clips spread fast. So did the questions about her future in basketball.Reporters asked whether another James child might follow in the footsteps of Bronny and Bryce or the man himself. LeBron answered with a smile and a clear message about where things stand at home. His response revealed that it has been focused on family priorities, and it offered a candid look at how the James household views Zhuri’s athletic journey.After the game, LeBron James made it clear that basketball is not part of the long-term plan for his daughter. He credited his wife, Savannah James, for drawing that line."She's a volleyball player, don't get my wife mad," LeBron said with a laugh. "My wife is done with basketball. She's done with it. [Zhuri's] a volleyball player, but she's been around the game for a while, so she does got good handles and she's good form, too.But my wife ain't playing that. Not another one. She says that's it."Zhuri’s primary focus is volleyball. LeBron’s sons are already immersed in competitive basketball, and the schedule that comes with the sport demands constant travel and year-round commitment. Savannah’s stance signals a desire for balance after years of living inside the game’s orbit.LeBron also shared how fatherhood has shaped him. "It's definitely softened me up over the last 11 years. I had two boys to begin with, but getting a little girl... man, it has definitely softened me up. It's a different type of love." He added, "It's a bit of softness with my daughter."Even with volleyball as her main sport, Zhuri looked comfortable with a ball in her hands. During warmups, she mirrored her father’s routine along the baseline. She attempted a backward free throw and eventually banked it in after adjusting her positioning. The crowd nearby reacted with smiles and applause.Check out Zhuri’s stunning trickshot:At one point, she tossed an alley oop toward the rim. LeBron finished it with a dunk. They exchanged a quick handshake and an “I love you” before he returned to his pregame routine.LeBron later called her a good luck charm, noting the Lakers are 1-0 with Zhuri in attendance on the road this season. For now, her future points toward volleyball.
Article Summary: LeBron James shared a moment with his daughter, Zhuri James, before a game. The 11-year-old showed off impressive basketball skills. LeBron said his wife, Savannah James, is done with basketball. Zhuri's primary focus is volleyball, and LeBron credited her for that.

Article Title: NBA injury update: Golden State Warriors drops major Stephen Curry news after missing 10 straight games
Article Text:
Stephen Curry. Image via: Getty Images

Golden State Warriors provides major Stephen Curry injury update



Stephen Curry addresses his injury



Stephen Curry of the Golden State Warriors (Image via Imagn Images)

The Golden State Warriors walked off the floor after a tough loss to the Los Angeles Lakers with more questions than answers. Their season continues to hinge on the health of Stephen Curry, who has not played since Jan. 30. The veteran guard remains sidelined with a right knee issue that has lingered for weeks, forcing the team to adjust on the fly.Golden State sits at 31-29, eighth in the Western Conference. At this point in time, the margin for error is thin. With Curry unavailable and the offense struggling to find rhythm, the Warriors released a fresh medical update that outlines the next phase of his recovery and what it means for the coming stretch.The Warriors confirmed Sunday that Stephen Curry will remain out for at least 10 more days. In an official statement, the team said:“Golden State Warriors guard Stephen Curry, who has missed the last 10 games due to patella-femoral pain syndrome/bone bruising in his right knee, was recently re-evaluated. The re-evaluation indicated that Curry continues to make progress. He will be re-evaluated again in 10 days,” read an official press release from the Warriors.A follow-up announcement provides a clear picture of his return timeline. “The re-evaluation concluded that Curry continues to make progress. He will be re-evaluated again in 10 days,” the announcement read.Curry has already missed 10 straight games. The Warriors have gone 4-6 during that stretch. Overall, they are 23-16 when he plays and 8-13 when he sits. He has appeared in 39 games this season, averaging 27.2 points on 46.8 percent shooting, along with 4.8 assists and 4.5 made threes per game.Because he has missed more than 21 games, Curry is no longer eligible for end-of-season awards. His earliest possible return now projects to March 13 at home against the Minnesota Timberwolves. Before that, the Golden State hosts the Los Angeles Clippers.Curry spoke with ESPN’s Malika Andrews during Saturday’s 129-101 loss and offered insight into his recovery."I'm feeling better," Curry said. "This is a weird one. It's kinda unpredictable how it will heal, but every day since All-Star Weekend it's been progress. That's all I can ask for. Hopefully I'll be back out there soon."He also addressed the balance between pain and caution."Once I get back on the court, it is a little bit of a pain-tolerance thing. But it's something that you don't want to have lingering because it can get worse."Curry has not resumed full-on-court work. Multiple MRIs revealed that he has not sustained any structural damage. However, as the soreness persists, head coach Steve Kerr has emphasized patience as the team manages the recovery.
Article Summary: Golden State Warriors confirm Stephen Curry will remain out for at least 10 more days. Multiple MRIs revealed that he has not sustained any structural damage. His earliest possible return now projects to March 13 at home against the Minnesota Timberwolves. Because he has missed more than 21 games, Curry is no longer eligible for end-of-season awards.

Article Title: LeBron James’ son Bronny James receives major Lakers news following win over Warriors
Article Text:
Bronny James. Image via: Michael Hickey/ Getty Images

Lakers recall Bronny James from G-League



Bronny and LeBron James. Image via: William Liang/ AP

Bronny James' stats in the South Bay Lakers



The Los Angeles Lakers are moving quickly as the playoff race tightens in the Western Conference. Fresh off a composed win over the Golden State Warriors, the focus has shifted to roster depth and rotation decisions. It has brought notable news on Sunday involving Bronny James.With 23 games left and Los Angeles holding a 35-24 record, every win will be crucial. As the Lakers prepare to host the Sacramento Kings, they have brought back a familiar young guard to strengthen the bench. The move comes at a critical point in the season, as Beonny was dominating in the Lakers’ G-League affiliate, South Bay Lakers.The Lakers recalled James from the G League's South Bay Lakers on Sunday, Dave McMenamin of ESPN.com reports.James will rejoin the parent club after being assigned to the G League on Monday. The second-year guard will be available for Sunday's game against the Kings, though he isn't guaranteed to see meaningful playing time.NBA legend LeBron James 's son, Bronny James has now followed a pattern similar to his rookie campaign. Selected 55th overall in the 2024 NBA Draft, the former USC guard has split time between the NBA roster and South Bay.Through 29 appearances this season, he has averaged 2.2 points, 1.2 assists and 0.5 rebounds in 7.3 minutes per game. He is shooting 40 percent from the field and 40.7 percent from three-point range.His NBA minutes have come in short stretches, often late in games or when the rotation has been stretched. Over the past five contests, including Saturday’s win over Golden State, he was assigned to the G League to continue his development. His last Lakers appearance came Feb. 12 against the Dallas Mavericks, when he logged two minutes.While his NBA numbers remain modest, his G League production has been impressive. In 11 games with the South Bay Lakers, all starts, James averaged 21.9 points, 5.5 assists, 5.1 rebounds, and 1.9 steals per game. He handled more on-ball duties and played extended minutes, a clear shift from his limited NBA role.The Lakers view him as a developmental guard who can provide backcourt depth during congested stretches. His improved decision-making as a secondary ball handler has stood out during recent assignments.Los Angeles hosts Sacramento at 9:30 p.m. ET on Sunday.
Article Summary: Lakers recall Bronny James from G-League affiliate South Bay Lakers. James will rejoin the parent club after being assigned to the G League on Monday. Second- year guard will be available for Sunday's game against the Sacramento Kings, though he isn't guaranteed to see meaningful playing time.

New Orleans Pelicans vs Los Angeles Clippers Game Outlook and What to Expect (March 1, 2026)

Thetravel to face theon, in a Western Conference game between two teams trying to steady uneven seasons. Both sides are outside the top group in the standings, so this game is more about staying in the mix than making a statement.Player availability is the main focus. Both teams have injuries that could affect rotations, scoring, and how they close the game. The result may come down to which team handles those gaps better over four quarters.The New Orleans Pelicans could be without Zion Williamson, who is listed as questionable with a right ankle sprain. He left early in their last win against the Jazz, and if he does not play, it removes a key scoring option inside.Yves Missi is also questionable with a calf issue.Trey Murphy III has been ruled out with a shoulder injury, and Dejounte Murray is still out while working his way back. That leaves New Orleans short on both ends of the floor.For the Clippers, Kawhi Leonard is available after dealing with an ankle issue. His return helps on both ends. He is expected to play alongside Ivica Zubac, giving Los Angeles a steady presence in the frontcourt.New Orleans continues to rely on a mix of young players and regular contributors.Pelicans key players include Zion Williamson (PF), Jordan Poole (SG), Jeremiah Fears (PG), Trey Murphy III (SF), Yves Missi (C), Saddiq Bey (SF), Herb Jones (SF), and Derik Queen (C).The Clippers have a more experienced group led by Kawhi Leonard (F), James Harden (G), Ivica Zubac (C), John Collins (F), Kris Dunn (PG), Brook Lopez (C), Derrick Jones (SF), and Nicolas Batum (F-G).These rosters reflect moves made during the 2025–26 season, with no former Pelicans players included.The Pelicans lead this matchup overall. If he does not play, the Clippers have the edge in structure and experience.Los Angeles comes in with a more stable lineup and fewer changes, which could matter. The Pelicans will need scoring from several players to stay in the game. Tip-off is set for 8:00 PM ET in Los Angeles.

Philadelphia 76ers vs Boston Celtics Key Storyline: Maxey vs Brown Takes Center Stage (March 1, 2026)

Thehead to TD Garden on March 1, 2026, to play thein an Atlantic Division game that matters for both teams in the standings. Boston comes in at 39-20 and leads the division, while Philadelphia is 33-26 and trying to hold its place in the playoff race.Both teams are dealing with roster issues, which changes how this game sets up. With several key players unavailable, the focus shifts to how well each side manages its rotations and handles the minutes across the lineup.Both teams are missing important players, and that will affect how the game is played.Boston is still without Jayson Tatum, who is recovering from an Achilles tear from last season’s playoffs. He has returned to practice but has not been cleared to play.Baylor Scheierman is listed as questionable because of a thumb fracture, which puts his availability in doubt.Philadelphia also has several absences. Joel Embiid is out with a right oblique strain and is expected to miss multiple games. Johni Broome remains sidelined after undergoing a partial meniscectomy on his lateral meniscus. Paul George is unavailable as he serves a suspension.With these players out, Tyrese Maxey takes on a larger role for the 76ers, while Boston relies on Jaylen Brown to lead the scoring.Both teams are expected to start adjusted lineups based on who is available.Derrick White and Jaylen Brown will handle most of Boston’s offense. For Philadelphia, Maxey will control scoring and playmaking responsibilities.Head-to-Head Trends Favor BostonBoston has the edge in this matchup over time. The Celtics lead the all-time regular season series 274-200, which works out to a 57.8% win rate.Recent results also favor Boston. Over the last five seasons, Philadelphia has gone 8-12 against them. The teams have split their earlier meetings this season, but Boston has scored 112.5 points per game in the more recent matchups.Philadelphia has stayed competitive but has clear gaps in its numbers. The team scores 116.2 points per game, which ranks 20th, and allows 116.0. They rank fifth in steals at 9.2 per game and third in free throw percentage at 81.4%. Rebounding is an issue, as they rank 28th with 43.4 per game. Maxey leads with 26.5 points and 7.2 assists.Boston has been steady across its rotation. Jaylen Brown leads with 29.1 points and 7.3 rebounds per game. Derrick White adds 13.2 points and 6.3 assists. Neemias Queta averages 8.7 points and 7.7 rebounds, while Sam Hauser contributes 11.3 points and 2.9 three-pointers per game. Even without Tatum, the team has kept its position near the top of the standings.With Tatum and Embiid out, attention shifts to Tyrese Maxey and Jaylen Brown. Both will have the ball often and set the pace for their teams.Boston has the advantage at home, but Philadelphia’s defense can create turnovers and slow the game down. Players like Neemias Queta and Kelly Oubre Jr. could have a role in deciding how the game plays out.
Article Summary: The Boston Celtics host the Philadelphia 76ers on March 1, 2026. The Celtics lead the all-time regular season series 274-200, which works out to a 57.8% win rate. Over the last five seasons, Philadelphia has gone 8-12 against them.
